sections.title: Foundational Synthesis — Operator Profile & Asset Quality
required_elements: Scale classification (major >1M oz/year, mid-tier 300K-1M oz, junior <300K oz)
Operational stage classification (producer, developer, explorer)
Asset portfolio mapping by jurisdiction with political risk tiering (Tier 1, 2, 3)
Management geological credibility and capital allocation discipline assessment

sections.title: Mine-Level Economics — The Cost Curve Position
required_elements: All-In Sustaining Cost (AISC) vs. gold price margin cushion analysis
AISC trajectory assessment (rising vs. falling trend drivers)
Head grade profile and trajectory analysis (high-grading risk)
Reserve & resource conversion tracking (Inferred → Indicated → Measured → Probable → Proven)
Mine life and reserve replacement cliff assessment (<8 years = replacement risk)
By-product credit assessment (silver, copper, zinc revenue stability)

sections.title: Gold Price Macro Framework
required_elements: Real interest rate sensitivity and central bank buying structural demand assessment
USD strength/weakness and ETF flow impact analysis
FCF impact modeling at $100/oz increments ($1,800, $1,900, $2,000, $2,100, $2,200)
Gold price floor identification (FCF turns negative threshold)

sections.title: Financial Logic & Valuation
required_elements: P/NAV valuation framework (1.0x NAV = fair for producers, 0.3-0.5x for developers)
EV/EBITDA valuation approach
FCF yield methodology
IRR-based probability-weighted NPV for development-stage companies
Shareholder return capacity assessment (dividend sustainability at $200/oz below spot)

sections.title: Pattern Matching & IDP Flagging
required_elements: Declining reserve grades (depletion or high-grading signal)
AISC rising faster than inflation (operational deterioration)
Acquisition-driven production growth guidance (empire-building risk)
Jurisdictional risk events (permit revocations, tax regime changes, community opposition)

sections.title: Investigation Tracks
required_elements: Mine-by-mine AISC decomposition and grade trajectory analysis
M&A pipeline analysis (potential targets, acquisition discipline assessment)
Exploration upside quantification (district-scale discovery potential)

structural_requirements: All sections present with markdown headings
Scale classification clearly stated (major, mid-tier, or junior)
AISC explicitly calculated and benchmarked against gold price with margin cushion
AISC trend analyzed with specific drivers (grade, cost inflation, infrastructure age)
Head grade trajectory documented with high-grading risk assessment
Reserve life calculated (reserves / annual production = years of mine life)
By-product credits quantified as percentage of net cash costs
Gold price scenario analysis at 5 price points with FCF turning negative identified
P/NAV or alternative valuation calculated with peer comparison
Shareholder return sustainability assessed at prices 5-10% below current spot
M&A track record evaluated for value creation/destruction
Exploration upside estimated with district-scale discovery risk/reward
Investigation tracks include mine-by-mine productivity data, M&A target list, and exploration results
